the_signal.headline: Token economics exposed: agentic AI costs exploding in production
the_signal.body: A new paper quantifies where tokens are actually consumed in agentic software engineering—and it's forcing GTM teams to recalculate CAC models. If you're selling AI agents or building with them, you need to understand token spend per execution stage or your margin math breaks down fast.

tool_drop.name: Oproxy
tool_drop.what: Browser-native tool to inspect and modify network traffic in real-time without proxy setup.
tool_drop.why: Critical for debugging AI agent behavior—you can now watch LLM requests, responses, and data flows directly from the client side instead of blind logging.

thread_of_day.title: I design with Claude more than Figma now
thread_of_day.takeaway: Design workflows are being replaced by agentic AI—teams shipping 2-3x faster by directing Claude to generate UI/UX rather than manual tooling, reshaping how product teams operate.

quote.text: 92% of C-suite leaders are fully confident in AI ROI. Yet 75% lack a governance framework and 58% have no clear owner of AI.

whats_moving.context: Enterprises are moving document automation and sensitive workflows onto private infrastructure (IONOS, Nextcloud) to meet data residency requirements—governance is now a feature, not a blocker.
Cross-system integration is becoming the unglamorous blocker to AI scaling; n8n's push on data mapping best practices signals this is where real deployment friction lives.
Silent hallucinations and invisible failures are forcing teams to build execution tracing layers—debugging AI agents is now table stakes for production deployments.

act_on_this: Calculate your AI agent's token spend per operation (input, inference, retry) and map it against your GTM unit economics—if cost per qualified lead is rising, token efficiency is your next lever, not model swaps.

numbers.stat: 75% of enterprises lack an AI governance framework despite 92% confidence in AI ROI
numbers.context: This gap represents massive GTM risk: your customers are deploying AI agents without controls, which means they'll blame you when hallucinations cause deal friction or compliance issues.
